payload.text:
Skapa en webbsida där studenter hittar extrajobb vid sidan av studierna. Sidan riktar sig till studenter och arbetsgivare. Målet är snabba matchningar, låg tröskel och tydlig struktur. Du designar för mobil först och sedan desktop.

Målgrupp
Du riktar dig till studenter mellan 18 och 30 år. De studerar på universitet, högskola eller yrkeshögskola. De söker deltidsjobb, timjobb och korta uppdrag. Arbetsgivare är små företag, restauranger, butiker, lager, eventbolag och privatpersoner.

Kärnfunktioner för studenter
Du låter studenten skapa en profil på under två minuter.
Profilen innehåller namn, ålder, studieort och utbildning.
Profilen visar tillgängliga tider per vecka.
Profilen visar erfarenhet i enkla val.
Profilen visar om studenten vill jobba fysiskt, online eller båda.

Du visar jobb i ett flöde.
Varje jobb visar lön per timme.
Varje jobb visar arbetstid och plats.
Varje jobb visar hur snabbt jobbet startar.

Du ger ett knapptryck för ansökan.
Ingen personligt brev krävs.
Ett klick skickar profilen direkt.

Du lägger till filter.
Filter för stad.
Filter för lön.
Filter för arbetstyp.
Filter för kväll, helg eller vardag.

Du visar matchningsgrad i procent.
Matchningen baseras på tider, plats och erfarenhet.

Kärnfunktioner för arbetsgivare
Du låter arbetsgivaren skapa konto snabbt.
De fyller i företagsnamn och kontaktperson.

De lägger upp jobb via ett enkelt formulär.
Formuläret har fasta svarsalternativ.
Inga långa texter krävs.

De ser sökande direkt.
De sorterar efter matchningsgrad.
De bokar intervju via chatt eller kalender.

Du visar kostnad tydligt.
Gratis för ett begränsat antal annonser.
Betalplan för fler annonser.

Smarta tillägg
Du bygger ett betygssystem.
Studenter betygsätter arbetsgivare.
Arbetsgivare betygsätter studenter.

Du lägger till verifiering.
Student verifierar studier via studentmejl.
Arbetsgivare verifierar organisationsnummer.

Du skapar en jobbtyp för snabba pass.
Pass på 3 till 6 timmar.
Bokning samma dag.

Du lägger till notiser.
Pushnotiser för nya jobb.
Pushnotiser för svar på ansökan.

Du visar statistik i profilen.
Antal jobb genomförda.
Svarstid från arbetsgivare.

Design och användarupplevelse
Du använder enkel färgskala.
Du använder tydliga knappar.
Du visar viktig info först.

Du minimerar text.
Du använder ikoner där det passar.

Du gör varje steg tydligt.
Registrering.
Matchning.
Ansökan.
Start.

Affärsidéer
Du tar betalt per publicerat jobb.
Du erbjuder abonnemang till företag.
Du säljer synlighet för jobbannonser.
Du erbjuder snabbmatch som premiumfunktion.

Teknisk riktning
Du bygger med fokus på snabb laddning.
Du optimerar för mobil.
Du använder enkel inloggning via mejl eller BankID.

Resultat
När du är klar har du en tydlig, snabb och fokuserad jobbsida.
Du hjälper studenter hitta jobb utan krångel.
Du hjälper arbetsgivare hitta personal snabbt.

**Initiating Project Conception**

I'm focusing on the React web application concept, envisioning "StudentJobb" as a potential name. The core idea is to bridge the gap between students aged 18-30 and employers, particularly small businesses and restaurants, who need part-time or short-term help. The goal is a Single Page Application (SPA).


**Detailing Feature Set**

I'm now diving into the specifics of the feature set. I've fleshed out the user profiles, availability tracking, and job feed. Thinking about one-click applications, and matching scores to streamline the user experience. For employers, I'm focusing on ease of account setup and applicant management tools, incorporating features like sorting by match score and chat/calendar options.


**Implementing Matching Logic**

I'm now implementing the matching algorithm. I'm leveraging Gemini to calculate a match score. I've broken down the project structure into essential components: metadata, entry points, routers, type definitions, and services. The core UI elements are taking shape. Student and employer views are well-defined. My simplified matching logic focuses on comparing tags. I'm leaning toward using a multi-step form for quick user profiles.


**Generating Content and Structure**

I'm expanding my focus on content creation and structural elements. I'm using Gemini-3-flash-preview to generate job descriptions. The interface should be in Swedish for the target demographic. I'm working on simulating real-time notifications with UI alerts and adding a placeholder for BankID. I've also added a "User Mode" switcher for easy demonstration and realistic mock data to drive the application. I am using Swedish terms throughout the application.

entries.description: App metadata including title, description and required permissions.
Main entry HTML file with Tailwind CSS support.
React entry point initializing the application.
Global TypeScript types and interfaces for the application.
Application constants and mock data.

Lucide icon components for easier reuse.

payload.text: fixa så att knapparna funkar och lägg till funktioner och om man är arbetsgivare eller ansökande. arbetsgivaren ska kunna lägga in kraven för jobbet samt arbetsförhållanden

**Developing Core Features**

I'm now prioritizing the critical functionality, specifically the role-switching feature. This will let users fluidly move between the Student and Employer perspectives within the StudentJobb application. My focus is to create a seamless experience for navigation.


**Refining Key Components**

I've been working intensely on the Student and Employer views. I am implementing the interactive job posting form, and the actual filtering logic is next. I am now refining the `App.tsx` and adding a new `JobForm.tsx` component, including mock data updates in `constants.tsx`. The student role will have an interactive "Apply" button and profile view. The employer will be able to post jobs, and view applicants.


**Prioritizing UI Elements**

I'm now focusing on the job posting form. I will make a simplified form with predefined options for key aspects. The bottom bar navigation should function for all tabs and roles. I will use buttons for job duration, type (physical/online), and shift, instead of free text. I'll focus on adding the `jobs` state and `activeTab` switching logic in `App.tsx`.


**Constructing Employer Features**

I am now focusing on the employer side's job posting form. I need to make sure the employer can specify job requirements and working conditions effectively. I will check the `process.env.API_KEY` in the Gemini service for match scoring, and also, make sure the "Post Job" button works.

I kommer nu att uppdatera applikationen för att göra den fullt funktionell. Jag lägger till ett formulär för arbetsgivare att posta jobb med fasta svarsalternativ, implementerar filtrering i jobbflödet och ser till att alla navigeringsknappar fungerar sömlöst.

### Specifikation av ändringar:

1.  **Arbetsgivarportal**: 
    *   Ett nytt formulär för att lägga upp jobb läggs till. Det använder "fasta svarsalternativ" för tid, lön och typ för att minimera textinmatning.
    *   Möjlighet att växla mellan "Dashboard" och "Skapa Jobb".
2.  **Studentflöde**: 
    *   Implementerar fungerande filter för stad, lön och arbetstyp.
    *   Snabba pass (3-6 timmar) markeras tydligt.
3.  **Navigation & Logik**: 
    *   Knappen för att växla mellan Student och Arbetsgivare görs mer framträdande för demo-syften.
    *   Navigeringsmenyn i botten kopplas ihop med de olika vyerna för båda rollerna.
4.  **Visuell design**: 
    *   Förbättrad responsivitet och tydligare knappar för mobilanvändare.

entries.description: Uppdaterar huvudkomponenten med tillstånd för jobb, filtrering och ett fungerande arbetsgivarformulär.

payload.text:
Funktioner för studenter
• Tidsblockering i kalender. Du markerar tentaperioder. Jobb visas inte då.
• Snabbstatus. Du sätter dig som tillgänglig eller upptagen.
• Favoritjobb. Du sparar jobb och får påminnelse före sista ansökningsdag.
• Löneöversikt. Du ser total intjänad lön per månad.
• Skattehjälp. Du får enkel info om skatt och fribelopp.
• CV-generator. Systemet skapar ett enkelt CV från profilen.
• Referenser. Du samlar omdömen efter varje avslutat jobb.
• Gruppjobb. Du söker jobb där flera studenter behövs samtidigt.

Funktioner för arbetsgivare
• Återkommande jobb. Du lägger upp samma pass varje vecka.
• Favoritstudenter. Du sparar studenter för framtida jobb.
• Snabbaccept. Du godkänner med ett klick.
• Ersättare. Du hittar ersättare vid sena avbokningar.
• Kontrakt. Systemet skapar ett enkelt digitalt avtal.
• Löneförslag. Plattformen visar rimlig timlön baserat på stad och jobbtyp.

Matchning och kvalitet
• Tillförlitlighetspoäng. Baserad på närvaro och omdömen.
• Avbokningshistorik. Syns tydligt i profilen.
• Prioritet för pålitliga användare. De visas högre i listor.
• Tidszonsskydd. Inga missförstånd vid distansjobb.

Kommunikation
• Inbyggd chatt. All kontakt sker på plattformen.
• Fördefinierade svar. Sparar tid för båda parter.
• Lässtatus på meddelanden.
• Automatiska påminnelser före arbetspass.

Trygghet och regler
• ID-kontroll för studenter.
• Ålderskontroll för vissa jobb.
• Rapportera problem direkt i appen.
• Supportchatt med snabba svar.
• Tydliga regler för sena avbokningar.

Plats och flexibilitet
• Karta med jobb nära dig.
• Resersättning markerad tydligt.
• Jobb på distans med verifierad arbetsgivare.
• Tillgänglighetsfilter för funktionsvariationer.

Gamification och engagemang
• Nivåsystem för aktiva studenter.
• Märken för punktlighet och bra omdömen.
• Veckomål. Antal pass eller intjänad summa.

Affärsutveckling
• Företagskonton för skolor och event.
• Samarbete med studentkårer.
• Rekryteringsspår för framtida heltidsjobb.
• White label-lösning för större organisationer.

Analys och insikter
• Insikter för studenter om efterfrågade jobb.
• Insikter för arbetsgivare om svarstid och intresse.
• Toppjobb per stad och period.

**Analyzing Feature Expansion**

I'm currently focused on the implications of expanding "StudentJobb." Specifically, I'm thinking through student features, like adding an availability calendar and time blocking functionality. This also involves integrating a tentaper for better organization. Next, I'll be looking into the corresponding employer features and how they'll interact.


**Expanding Platform Capabilities**

I've been meticulously cataloging the features to expand "StudentJobb." This includes student tools like an availability status and a CV generator, along with employer options such as recurring jobs and salary suggestions. I'm also carefully considering matching and quality enhancements, as well as trust and safety measures. Furthermore, I've outlined new communication functionalities, plus new location/flexibility and gamification features.


**Integrating New Features**

I'm now integrating new features into the core application. Specifically, I'm updating interfaces in `types.ts` to include reliability scores and other profile data. In `App.tsx`, I'm expanding state management for favorites, chat, and more, as well as new tabs for core student functionality. I'm also adding logic for reliability, levels, favorite flags, and badges. Finally, I'm keeping the core design mobile-first and focusing on responsive UI elements.


**Implementing Salary Suggestions**

I'm now integrating salary suggestions using Gemini, dependent on job titles and locations. I'm focusing on simplifying both student and employer onboarding. This means a quick two-minute profile, availability settings, and simple choices for students. Employers get fast accounts and simple forms. I'm also implementing a new mock chat and calendar view in the app and an economy view for students.


**Outlining UI Components**

I'm currently outlining the UI components for students and employers. For students, I'm focusing on the Feed, Messages, Economy, Profile, and Calendar views. Employers get the Dashboard, Post, and Chat sections. I'm adding specific icons to `Icons.tsx` and planning a placeholder map view. I'm also considering the CV generator and reliability score visualizations. Now, I'm diving deeper into implementation.

I will now implement the requested features, expanding "StudentJobb" into a comprehensive platform with chat, calendar management, gamification, and economic overviews for students, alongside advanced management tools for employers.

### Specification of Updates:

1.  **Student Enhancements**:
    *   **Gamification**: Added `level` and `badges` to the profile. Completed jobs increase level.
    *   **Economy View**: A new "Ekonomi" tab showing total earned, tax thresholds (fribelopp), and earnings progress.
    *   **Calendar**: A "Kalender" section for blocking time (e.g., exams) which prevents job matches during those periods.
    *   **CV Generator**: A feature in the profile to "generate" (preview) a professional summary.
    *   **Reliability Score**: A percentage displayed on profiles based on attendance.

2.  **Employer Enhancements**:
    *   **Recurring Jobs**: Option to mark jobs as "Återkommande".
    *   **Favorite Students**: Ability to "star" applicants for future needs.
    *   **Salary Insights**: Using Gemini to suggest a competitive hourly rate based on job type and city.
    *   **Quick Accept**: One-click button to hire an applicant.

3.  **Communication & Safety**:
    *   **In-app Chat**: A simplified chat interface for coordinating pass details.
    *   **Map View**: A placeholder map integration (using geolocation permission) to find local shifts.

4.  **Visual Improvements**:
    *   **Progress Bars**: For levels and reliability.
    *   **Badges**: Visual icons for "Punktlig", "Expert", etc.
    *   **Enhanced Navigation**: 5-icon bottom navigation for students (Feed, Chat, Map, Economy, Profile).

entries.description: Expand interfaces to support gamification, reliability, economy, and favorites.
Update mock data with new fields.

entries.description: Add new utility icons.
Add salary suggestion logic using Gemini.
Major overhaul of the main App component with new tabs, logic, and features.

payload.text: Lägg till ett textfält för 'Arbetsförhållanden' i jobbannonsen som arbetsgivaren kan fylla i. Visa detta fält i JobCard komponenten.

**Focusing on Form Fields**

I'm now zeroing in on integrating the "Arbetsförhållanden" field (Working Conditions) into the employer's job posting form. My current focus involves how to best incorporate this new text field and ensure its accurate rendering within the `JobCard` component for student view. I am considering best practices for field naming and data handling.


**Refining the Type Definition**

I've just started to update the `Job` interface within `types.ts` to integrate the `workingConditions` field. My plan is to reflect this change across the application, adding this new field into the `newJob` state in `App.tsx` and the corresponding form input. Next on my list is to include this field into the `handleCreateJob` function, ensuring everything stays consistent. I'll then move on to updating `JobCard.tsx` to display this information and think about updating some mock data, as well.

entries.description: Add workingConditions field to the Job interface.
Update mock jobs to include working conditions.

Add input for working conditions in the job creation form.

payload.text: Modify the JobCard component to include a new section for 'Employer Requirements'. This section should display an array of strings representing specific requirements from the employer, similar to how 'job.requirements' is displayed. Add a new field 'employerRequirements' to the Job interface in types.ts and update MOCK_JOBS in constants.tsx with sample data for this new field.

**Implementing New Feature: Employer Requirements**

I'm currently working on incorporating the "Employer Requirements" section into the `JobCard` component. This involves modifying the `Job` interface in `types.ts` to include `employerRequirements: string[]`. I've also begun to update the `MOCK_JOBS` data in `constants.tsx` to reflect this new field. The goal is to ensure a smooth integration that accurately reflects employer expectations.


**Revising Implementation Strategy**

I've revised the plan to more accurately reflect the user's instructions. I'll add the `employerRequirements: string[]` field to the `Job` interface in `types.ts`. Then, I'll update the `MOCK_JOBS` data in `constants.tsx` with some sample data for this new field. Finally, the `JobCard` component will be modified to display these requirements. I'm also going to update `App.tsx` to handle this new field in job creation. This should improve the overall integrity of the app!


**Prioritizing Task Scope**

I've realized the direct instructions are to modify `JobCard` to include a section for "Employer Requirements". I will also add the `employerRequirements` field to the `Job` interface in `types.ts`, and update `MOCK_JOBS` data in `constants.tsx`, in that order. I think this will best maintain the user's focus on the initial request.

entries.description: Add employerRequirements field to the Job interface.
Update MOCK_JOBS with employerRequirements data.

Initialize employerRequirements in the new job form state.

payload.text: Update the employer job posting form in App.tsx. Add a textarea field for 'workingConditions'. Ensure this new field is included when a new job is created and that it is correctly passed to the Job object.

**Reviewing Job Posting Form**

I've been reviewing the job posting form in `App.tsx` and have confirmed the existing `workingConditions` field. I need to ensure it's correctly managed within the application's state and is included when a new job is created.


**Validating Working Conditions Inclusion**

I've been examining the `App.tsx` file to address the working conditions inclusion. It seems the user already implemented a textarea for this, which is good. My immediate concern is that `workingConditions` is present in the `textarea` and if it is correctly updated in the `onChange`. I need to verify that it's being properly managed in the `newJob` state and passed along when a new job is constructed and ensure that no data is lost during the transfer.


**Refining Form Integration**

I'm now refining the job posting form in `App.tsx` for robustness. The existing code correctly handles the `workingConditions` field in the `textarea` using `onChange`. I've confirmed that the `newJob` state (which is a `Partial<Job>`) includes the data, and it is correctly included when the job is submitted. To increase the robustness, I will also add the `value` property to the `textarea` in the upcoming update. The `types.ts` is confirmed to include the field as well.


**Explicitly Managing State**

I'm now making the textarea a controlled component in `App.tsx` and explicitly including the `workingConditions` field when creating a new job to ensure full functionality and address the explicit user request. I've also verified that the `types.ts` file includes the necessary `workingConditions?: string;` field. While the spread operator in `handleCreateJob` works, I'll be more explicit to solidify the flow. I need to make sure I am fully addressing the user's needs.


**Solidifying Field Integrity**

I'm now focusing on the `workingConditions` field in `App.tsx`. I see the user already added a textarea, which is great. I will add the missing `value={newJob.workingConditions}` attribute to make it a controlled component and maintain its state. I will keep `employerRequirements` in mind but will not address it. I'm prioritizing the explicit request for the `workingConditions` field.


**Finalizing Field Implementation**

I'm now fully implementing the `workingConditions` field in `App.tsx` by making the textarea a controlled component and incorporating the `value` attribute, which was the final step needed. I'm now certain the field is correctly managed in the form's state and passed to the `Job` object. Now I'll reset the form after the user submits. I've also verified that the `JobCard` already displays the `workingConditions`.


**Implementing Full Functionality**

I'm now fully focusing on ensuring the user's explicit request is met and have updated `App.tsx` and now added a text area and its corresponding attribute and ensured the information flows properly, addressing the specific requirements. I've also verified that this is complete. I'll make sure to get the next update in on time.

entries.description: Uppdaterar formuläret för att skapa jobb med ett kontrollerat textfält för arbetsförhållanden och säkerställer att all data inkluderas vid skapande.
